evals.expected_output: A review identifying: (1) LanguageModelSession in the ViewModel violates the infrastructure boundary, (2) user input (userName) embedded in instructions is a prompt injection risk, (3) free-form text parsing instead of @Generable is fragile, (4) try? silently swallows errors. Each finding should include a before/after code fix.
assertions: The review flags that LanguageModelSession must not be in the ViewModel
The review flags that userName embedded in instructions is a prompt injection risk
The review flags that split/map text parsing should be replaced with @Generable
The review flags that try? silently swallows LLM-specific errors
Each finding includes a concrete before/after Swift code fix
The review ends with a prioritized summary

evals.expected_output: A review identifying: (1) availability check doesn't handle .modelNotReady (transient) vs .deviceNotEligible (permanent) differently, (2) a new LanguageModelSession is created on every summarize() call instead of being reused, (3) no prewarm() call. With before/after fixes.
assertions: The review flags that .modelNotReady is transient and should trigger a retry, unlike .deviceNotEligible which is permanent
The review flags that a new LanguageModelSession is created on every call instead of being reused
The review recommends prewarm() to reduce first-token latency
Each finding includes a before/after Swift code fix

evals.expected_output: A review identifying: (1) no error handling for guardrailViolation, exceededContextWindowSize, or unsupportedLanguageOrLocale, (2) WorkoutPlan has unused/unnecessary properties (historicalBackground, difficultyRating) that cost tokens without adding value, (3) no @Guide constraints on any property, (4) summary placed before exercises hurts output quality (property ordering). With before/after fixes.
assertions: The review flags missing error handling for LLM-specific errors (guardrailViolation, exceededContextWindowSize)
The review flags that historicalBackground and difficultyRating are unnecessary properties that cost tokens
The review recommends adding @Guide constraints to at least one property
The review mentions that property ordering affects generation quality (summary should come after content it summarizes)
Each finding includes a before/after Swift code fix
